channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Tanzania's Ambassador Urged to Enhance Tourism Ties with Korea
item.description: The article discusses a call from a Tanzanian official, Kijaji, to the embassy to strengthen tourism relations between Tanzania and Korea. While the provided text lacks detailed content, the core mess...

item.title: Ontario Provincial Police Disrupt Major Cross-Border Drug Smuggling Operation with $139 Million in Seizures
item.description: Ontario Provincial Police, in collaboration with Windsor Police Service and other law enforcement agencies, announced a significant disruption of a sophisticated cross-border drug smuggling network. A...

item.title: Detroit Woman Sues Taco Bell Over Cyclosporiasis Outbreak Linked to Lettuce
item.description: A Detroit woman has detailed her severe ordeal after contracting cyclosporiasis from contaminated lettuce at a Taco Bell, experiencing up to 30 to 40 bathroom trips daily. Ella-Salone Jones, who was h...

item.title: Trump administration seeks to rename New York Penn Station as part of broader infrastructure plans
item.description: The Trump administration has reignited discussions about renaming New York Penn Station in honor of former President Donald Trump, according to a letter from Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy. The l...

item.title: Congressional Divisions Over Iran Conflict Highlight Political Frustration
item.description: The US House of Representatives, led by Republicans, passed a resolution urging President Donald Trump to halt military action against Iran without congressional approval, reflecting growing bipartisa...

item.title: Tanzania seeks to strengthen tourism ties with Korea through embassy support

item.title: Sonam Wangchuk concludes 26-day hunger strike amid political reactions and ongoing protests
item.description: Activist Sonam Wangchuk ended his 26-day hunger strike at Medanta Hospital in Gurugram on July 23, 2026, with the presence of Union Ministers JP Nadda and Jitendra Singh. The decision followed negotia...

item.title: Manitoba Families Grieve After One of Two Missing Brothers Found in Nutimik Lake
item.description: The parents of two young brothers who drowned in the fast-moving currents of Nutimik Lake in Manitoba are enduring 'unimaginable pain' as search efforts continue for their 13-year-old son, Aaron Au Ra...

item.title: Australia faces tariffs amid US-Iran tensions and fuel supply concerns
item.description: The article details Australia's response to escalating tensions between the United States and Iran, highlighting a new Trump policy imposing a 12.5% tariff on Australian exports. Deputy Prime Minister...

item.title: Plane Skids Off Runway at Benin Airport Due to Heavy Rain
item.description: A plane flying from Enugu skidded off the runway after landing at Benin Airport on Thursday, July 23, 2026, due to torrential rainfall. The incident initially raised fears of a crash, but the Nigerian...

item.title: India's youth-led protests against government accountability enter fourth day
item.description: The unprecedented expression of dissent against India's government, led by Prime Minister Narendra Modi, has persisted for four days as the youth-led 'Cockroach' protest movement continues. The Guardi...

item.title: Commonwealth Games 2026 Opening Ceremony in Glasgow: A Celebration of Unity and Sport
item.description: The Commonwealth Games 2026 opening ceremony in Glasgow marked a significant moment as 74 nations gathered for the event. The ceremony, hosted at the Hydro venue, featured a vibrant display of nationa...

item.title: Trump Replaces Expiring Tariffs With Forced Labour Duties
item.description: The Trump administration is imposing new tariffs of 10% and 12.5% on 60 trading partners, including the European Union, over allegations of lax enforcement of forced labour bans. This move replaces ex...

item.title: ICC Dismisses War Crimes Case Against Sudan Rebel Due to Evidence Concerns
item.description: The International Criminal Court (ICC) has decided to drop the war crimes case against Sudanese rebel Abdallah Banda, who was accused of attacking African Union peacekeepers in Darfur nearly two decad...

item.title: Comelec warns candidates against premature campaigning in Dasmariñas special polls
item.description: The Commission on Elections (Comelec) has issued a warning to candidates in the special elections of Dasmariñas, Cavite, prohibiting premature campaigning before the official campaign period starts on...

item.title: Commonwealth Games Opening Ceremony: Royal Arrival and Team Australia's Grand Entrance
item.description: The Commonwealth Games opening ceremony in Glasgow featured a unique and entertaining twist as King Charles III and Queen Camilla arrived in a Dr Who Tardis, delighting the crowd. This creative choice...

item.title: Centre appoints new secretaries in education and other ministries as part of a major bureaucratic reshuffle
item.description: The Union government carried out a significant bureaucratic reshuffle on Thursday by announcing 13 secretary-level appointments across various ministries and departments. Among the most closely watche...

item.title: Nigerian Army Declares Private Wanted Over Alleged Illegal Supply of Military Uniforms to Criminal Groups
item.description: The Nigerian Army has declared one of its personnel, Private Mohammed Yusuf Amutu, wanted over allegations that he unlawfully sold and supplied military uniforms to terrorists and other criminal eleme...

item.title: House Oversight Committee Demands Investigation into Trump Family's Ties with MAGA Brothers Facing Serious Charges
